findings.message: New `redis` scrape job targets `redis-exporter:9121`, but no `redis-exporter` service exists in any Compose file. `redis` itself is defined in `services.yml` (prod) and exposes no Prometheus metrics endpoint on 9121 — only the bare `redis:7-alpine` server plus a dev port map. The exporter that would translate Redis stats into the `redis_*` metric series is absent (cf. the existing `postgres-exporter` in `observability.yml:142`), so this target is unroutable and the job will sit permanently `DOWN`. Redis is a prod runtime surface; adding the scrape job without the exporter leaves it effectively unobserved.
findings.suggestion: Add a `redis-exporter` service (e.g. `oliver006/redis_exporter`) to `infra/compose/observability.yml` on the `backend` network, pointed at the `redis` service, before (or with) this scrape job — mirror how `postgres-exporter` backs the `postgres` scrape job.

findings.message: The new `redis` scrape job has no matching Prometheus alert rule. `infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/` contains alertmanager/otel_collector/postgres/prometheus/slo rule files but nothing for Redis (no `redis_up`, memory, or eviction alerts). A scraped prod surface with no alert rule means failures (Redis down, OOM, evictions) page no one.
findings.suggestion: Add `infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml` with at least a `RedisDown` (on `redis_up == 0` / target down) alert and memory/eviction alerts, routed through an Alertmanager receiver, paralleling `postgres_alerts.yml`.

findings.message: The new `redis` scrape job has no matching Grafana dashboard. `infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/` ships api-slo/otel-collector/postgres/prometheus dashboards but no redis dashboard, so the `redis_*` series this job collects will not be visualized anywhere.
findings.suggestion: Add `infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json` covering the key `redis_*` metrics (connected clients, memory, hit rate, evictions, replication), mirroring the existing `postgres.json` dashboard.
